pattern: Ordering is the whole trick: the lowest common ancestor of two BST nodes is the first node whose value falls between them, so a single root-to-leaf walk finds it without any parent bookkeeping.
hints: You never need to search both subtrees — the BST property tells you which side both targets live on.
Walk down from the root: if both values are smaller go left, if both are larger go right.
The first node where the two values split (or one equals the node) is the answer; return it immediately.

edge_inputs.description: targets in different subtrees of the root
one target is the ancestor of the other
smallest possible tree, parent and child
answer is an interior node, not the root
right-skewed chain where the answer equals the shallower target
both targets deep in the left subtree

stress.description: random BST built by insertion with two distinct target values
